question: Should law enforcement agents wear body cameras?
agent1_stance:
Your stance:
You support law enforcement wearing body cameras.
You believe body cameras improve accountability and reduce misconduct, with research showing significant reductions in use-of-force incidents and Miami-Dade County seeing a 19% reduction in physical force and 74% drop in civil cases.
You believe body cameras are a powerful tool in domestic violence cases, with immediate recordings capturing victim injuries and statements that strengthen prosecutions and encourage guilty pleas.
You believe body cameras have strong public support of 90%+ and enhance transparency, aid officer training, and represent meaningful reform to rebuild trust between police and communities.
agent2_stance:
Your stance:
You oppose requiring law enforcement to wear body cameras.
You believe body cameras are unreliable and prohibitively expensive, with programs like Baltimore's tripling from $11.3 million to $35.1 million and many smaller departments abandoning them due to rising costs.
You believe body cameras cause invasive privacy violations, exposing sensitive medical conditions, traumatizing abuse victims, endangering informants, and enabling facial recognition abuse.
You believe body cameras create officer safety and mental health risks, with assaults on officers increasing 14% when cameras were present and constant surveillance creating psychological stress.
